articles.body:
Over wasn’t always a word used to describe Lio Rush. Frankly, he struggled at first like all young wrestlers trying to make it do. Lio first appeared on the scene in 2014 working heavily in the independent scene before appearing on Ring of Honor and WWE, eventually he found a comfortable spot with All Elite Wrestling for the time being. While continuing to build his character he was unfortunately sidelined due to a shoulder injury in 2021 during the Casino Battle Royale match that took place at AEW’s All or Nothing. Contemplating his future and career path Rush retired early then came back months later following a roster opportunity from AEW on a one year deal. After a year was up he found a temporary home with New Japan Pro-Wrestling and Impact Wrestling for a few years before returning to All Elite thus where he’s begun to evolve into a terrifyingly cool character.

 In the beginning of March 2026 Rush started to flash his alter ego that he’s kept in his back pocket since the independent scene, “Blackheart” started to appear, then leading to successful interactions throughout the roster and a Ring of Honor World Television Championship on June 13th. Not wasting much time, he turned face to help wreck Don Callis and MJF’s plans in AEW’s 2026 Forbidden Door. Shortly after winning his title and beginning “*the reign*” he chose to separate himself from Action Andretti and CRU, but what's really pushing Lio? “He knows” he might tell you, “It’s fine” he might say, but who is *He* and what is fine? Lio isn’t quite up for revealing the truth because of the struggle to interview or get a full sentence from him. Although this character is quite dark, Lio’s future from here on seems to be burning bright.
articles.title: Lio Rush and He who pushes the Blackheart
articles.excerpt: Lio Rush has been catching heat lately and wrestling fans can't get enough of his eerie, reckless character development.
